steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: After the noun "Pascal," the blank introduces a modifying phrase before the main verb "is." This modifier must describe Pascal using a reduced relative clause — the compressed form of "which is ______ in software development and educational instruction."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'to use': An infinitive. "Pascal, to use in software development" implies purpose, not a descriptive modifier, and creates an ungrammatical reduced clause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'used': A past participle. Stands in for "which is used," forming the correct reduced relative clause: "Pascal, used in software development and educational instruction, is not an object-oriented language." ✓
  • Choice C — 'the use': A noun phrase. Inserting a noun between commas makes "Pascal, the use in software development" — an appositive that misidentifies Pascal as a use rather than a language. ✗
  • Choice D — 'using': A present participle. This would imply Pascal is actively using software development, which reverses the meaning — Pascal is the thing used, not the user. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
